title: Cervaria

The plants within this genus are typically annual or biennial herbs with a variety of growth habits. Cervaria species often have small, white or purple flowers arranged in umbels. They are often found growing in dry, rocky areas and are sometimes used as ornamentals or in traditional medicine.

intro: Cervaria is a genus of flowering plants in the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as broomrapes, which are parasitic plants that derive their nutrients from surrounding plant tissues. The health benefits of Cervaria include its potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate various health conditions such as infections, wounds, and oxidative stress.
